TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}
TEXT.source: maqinatsap a se Qalu (Qatsiɫay village, p.162)

S.FORM: ka sitsuayan masengats a se Pungudan a vaik a paikinuriɫ tua se Qalu.
S.PHON: ka siʦuajan masəŋaʦ a sə puŋudan a vaik a paikinuriʎ tua sə qaɭu.
S.FORM: ka sicuayan masengac a se Pungudan a vaik a paikinurilj tua se Qalu.
S.PHON: ka siʦuajan masəŋaʦ a sə puŋudan a vaik a paikinuRiʎ tua sə qaɣu.
S.TRANSL: Once upon a time the Pungudan people got tired of going to give a tribute of dried meat to the Qalu people.

S.FORM: manu limutseng a se Qalu; mirava, sa vaik a sema Pungudan a maqinatsap.
S.PHON: manu ɭimuʦəŋ a sə qaɭu; mirava, sa vaik a səma puŋudan a maqinaʦap.
S.FORM: manu limuceng a se Qalu; mirava, sa vaik a sema Pungudan a maqinacap.
S.PHON: manu ɣimuʦəŋ a sə qaɣu; miRava, sa vaik a səma puŋudan a maqinaʦap.
S.TRANSL: So the Qalu people were angry, and got ready and went to Pungudan to hunt heads.

S.FORM: djemaɫun tiamadju.
S.PHON: ɉəmaʎun tiamaɉu.
S.FORM: djemaljun tiamadju.
S.PHON: ɉəmaʎun tiamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: When they arrived,

S.FORM: kiqiɫa i pasulaulauz, a naremaung tua se Pungudan.
S.PHON: kiqiʎa i pasuɭauɭauz, a narəmauŋ tua sə puŋudan.
S.FORM: kiqilja i pasulaulauz, a naremaung tua se Pungudan.
S.PHON: kiqiʎa i pasuɣauɣauz, a naRəmauŋ tua sə puŋudan.
S.TRANSL: they hid below the village, ready to ambush the Pungudan people.

S.FORM: manu ɫengɫengan a bulabulay a vavayan.
S.PHON: manu ʎəŋʎəŋan a buɭabuɭaj a vavajan.
S.FORM: manu ljengljengan a bulabulay a vavayan.
S.PHON: manu ʎəŋʎəŋan a buɣabuɣaj a vavajan.
S.TRANSL: But they saw a beautiful girl,

S.FORM: qau matjatjumatjumaɫ tiamadju tu uzai a bulabulay a vavayan.
S.PHON: qau macacumacumaʎ tiamaɉu tu uzai a buɭabuɭaj a vavajan.
S.FORM: qau matjatjumatjumalj tiamadju tu uzai a bulabulay a vavayan.
S.PHON: qau maʦaʦumaʦumaʎ tiamaɉu tu uzai a buɣabuɣaj a vavajan.
S.TRANSL: and told one another that there was a beautiful girl.

S.FORM: qau maɫavar tiamadju: "iya ḍi.
S.PHON: qau maʎavar tiamaɉu: "ija ɖi.
S.FORM: qau maljavar tiamadju: "iya dri.
S.PHON: qau maʎavaR tiamaɉu: "ija ɖi.
S.TRANSL: And they decided: “Well now,

S.FORM: mayanga qemtsi a se Pungudan.
S.PHON: majaŋa qəmʦi a sə puŋudan.
S.FORM: mayanga qemci a se Pungudan.
S.PHON: majaŋa qəmʦi a sə puŋudan.
S.TRANSL: don’t kill the Pungudan people after all.

S.FORM: nangaq a vaik itjen a kivangavang tazua bulabulay a vavayan," aya tiamadju.
S.PHON: naŋaq a vaik icən a kivaŋavaŋ tazua buɭabuɭaj a vavajan," aja tiamaɉu.
S.FORM: nangaq a vaik itjen a kivangavang tazua bulabulay a vavayan," aya tiamadju.
S.PHON: naŋaq a vaik iʦən a kivaŋavaŋ tazua buɣabuɣaj a vavajan," aja tiamaɉu.
S.TRANSL: Let’s go and court the beautiful girl,” they said.

S.FORM: qau sualapen a tjakit, sa kelayan tua djaraɫap i tsasaw.
S.PHON: qau suaɭapən a cakit, sa kəɭajan tua ɉaraʎap i ʦasaw.
S.FORM: qau sualapen a tjakit, sa kelayan tua djaraljap i casaw.
S.PHON: qau suaɣapən a ʦakit, sa kəɣajan tua ɉaRaʎap i ʦasaw.
S.TRANSL: They took off their swords and hung them on a banyan tree outside.

S.FORM: mavukuts a djaraɫap tua kinaliaw nua tjakit.
S.PHON: mavukuʦ a ɉaraʎap tua kinaɭiaw nua cakit.
S.FORM: mavukuc a djaraljap tua kinaliaw nua tjakit.
S.PHON: mavukuʦ a ɉaRaʎap tua kinaɣiaw nua ʦakit.
S.TRANSL: The tree was bent over because there were so many swords.

S.FORM: qau sa vaik tiamadju a kivangavang a sema tjua bulabulay a vavayan.
S.PHON: qau sa vaik tiamaɉu a kivaŋavaŋ a səma cua buɭabuɭaj a vavajan.
S.FORM: qau sa vaik tiamadju a kivangavang a sema tjua bulabulay a vavayan.
S.PHON: qau sa vaik tiamaɉu a kivaŋavaŋ a səma ʦua buɣabuɣaj a vavajan.
S.TRANSL: So they went to the beautiful girl’s place to court her.

S.FORM: qau sa pakaivi anga azua bulabulay a vavayan.
S.PHON: qau sa pakaivi aŋa azua buɭabuɭaj a vavajan.
S.FORM: qau sa pakaivi anga azua bulabulay a vavayan.
S.PHON: qau sa pakaivi aŋa azua buɣabuɣaj a vavajan.
S.TRANSL: And (eventually) the beautiful girl was given in marriage (to one of them).

S.FORM: manu djemaɫun i Qalu, sa muri kisudjuan.
S.PHON: manu ɉəmaʎun i qaɭu, sa muri kisuɉuan.
S.FORM: manu djemaljun i Qalu, sa muri kisudjuan.
S.PHON: manu ɉəmaʎun i qaɣu, sa muRi kisuɉuan.
S.TRANSL: So they reached Qalu, and were going to make love.

S.FORM: manu neka nu nimadju; uzai a iku.
S.PHON: manu nəka nu nimaɉu; uzai a iku.
S.FORM: manu neka nu nimadju; uzai a iku.
S.PHON: manu nəka nu nimaɉu; uzai a iku.
S.TRANSL: But she had no private parts, and she had a tail.

S.FORM: qau "kudain anga tsu, a neka nu nimadju?" aya se Qalu.
S.PHON: qau "kudain aŋa ʦu, a nəka nu nimaɉu" aja sə qaɭu.
S.FORM: qau "kudain anga cu, a neka nu nimadju?" aya se Qalu.
S.PHON: qau "kudain aŋa ʦu, a nəka nu nimaɉu" aja sə qaɣu.
S.TRANSL: “What’s the matter with her, with no private parts?” said the Qalu people.

S.FORM: manu djemaɫun tua sikapuḍuan nua vatu.
S.PHON: manu ɉəmaʎun tua sikapuɖuan nua vatu.
S.FORM: manu djemaljun tua sikapudruan nua vatu.
S.PHON: manu ɉəmaʎun tua sikapuɖuan nua vatu.
S.TRANSL: Then the time came for dogs to be on heat;

S.FORM: mintu uzai anga a nimadju.
S.PHON: mintu uzai aŋa a nimaɉu.
S.FORM: mintu uzai anga a nimadju.
S.PHON: mintu uzai aŋa a nimaɉu.
S.TRANSL: and then she had her private parts.

S.FORM: sa kivadaqi anga azua vavayan tu tima kama, tu tima kina.
S.PHON: sa kivadaqi aŋa azua vavajan tu tima kama, tu tima kina.
S.FORM: sa kivadaqi anga azua vavayan tu tima kama, tu tima kina.
S.PHON: sa kivadaqi aŋa azua vavajan tu tima kama, tu tima kina.
S.TRANSL: So the woman was asked who were her father and mother.

S.FORM: qau tjemumaɫ azua vavayan: "tsautsau a ku kina; vatu a ku kama," aya tjemumaɫ.
S.PHON: qau cəmumaʎ azua vavajan: "ʦauʦau a ku kina; vatu a ku kama," aja cəmumaʎ.
S.FORM: qau tjemumalj azua vavayan: "caucau a ku kina; vatu a ku kama," aya tjemumalj.
S.PHON: qau ʦəmumaʎ azua vavajan: "ʦauʦau a ku kina; vatu a ku kama," aja ʦəmumaʎ.
S.TRANSL: And she told them: “My mother is human; my father is a dog,” she said.

S.FORM: qau nu puaɫak azua vavayan, ḍusaḍusa nu puaɫak a kintaɫ.
S.PHON: qau nu puaʎak azua vavajan, ɖusaɖusa nu puaʎak a kintaʎ.
S.FORM: qau nu pualjak azua vavayan, drusadrusa nu pualjak a kintalj.
S.PHON: qau nu puaʎak azua vavajan, ɖusaɖusa nu puaʎak a kintaʎ.
S.TRANSL: And when she bore children she bore twins.
